title: The Comeback Story
summary: How pressing pause reset priorities, made space for family, and reignited the work worth doing.
content: Sometimes the bravest thing you can do is stop. After sprinting in every direction, I hit pause to reset priorities, reconnect with family, and remember why I started building in the first place.

This episode kicks off the new Finding Rad era—one where intention beats hustle. I unpack what changed, what stayed, and how I'm designing a life that leaves room for curiosity, adventure, and the people who matter most.
If you're rebuilding, rebooting, or just rethinking your path, this one's for you.

title: Designing the Adventure Flask
summary: From scribbles to prototypes—an inside look at turning feedback into field-ready gear.
content: Great gear starts with solving a real problem. In this episode I share the research behind the Adventure Flask—talking to customers, testing assumptions, and gathering the constraints that shaped the final form.
You'll see early sketches, the tools I use to iterate quickly, and how I decide which features make the cut. It's messy, hands-on, and exactly why I love building physical products.
I also walk through the feedback loops we use at Hibear to make sure the gear works as hard in the field as it does in your imagination.

title: Shipping Without the Burnout
summary: Systems and guardrails that keep the projects shipping while energy stays high.
content: Momentum beats perfection. This episode is the playbook I use to keep projects moving forward while keeping energy tanks full.
I cover weekly planning rituals, the tools that make collaboration easier, and the guardrails I use to protect creativity (and my calendar).
If you're trying to ship consistently without burning out, you'll find a few experiments worth stealing.
